title: How to Fix the #VALUE! Error in Excel
dek: The #VALUE! error means a formula is trying to do math with something that is not a number — usually text or a stray space in a cell. The fix is to find the offending cell and clean it, or use a function that ignores text. Here is how.

seo.metaTitle: How to Fix the #VALUE! Error in Excel
seo.metaDescription: The #VALUE! error means a formula got text where it expected a number. Here is what causes it and how to find and fix it in Excel.

sources.label: Microsoft Support — How to correct a #VALUE! error

richText: #VALUE! is Excel's way of saying "I can't do this math." It almost always means a formula ran into text, a space, or the wrong kind of value where it expected a number.

items: Adding or multiplying a cell that contains text, like =A2+B2 where B2 holds "n/a".
A number stored as text, often with a hidden space in front of it.
A date entered in a format Excel does not recognize.
A function given the wrong type of argument.

body.text: Step 1: Find the cell causing it

richText: Click the cell with the error and look at the formula. Check every cell it references for text or spaces. A quick test: type =ISNUMBER(B2) next to a suspect cell — if it returns FALSE, that cell is text, not a number.

body.text: Step 2: Clean or work around it

items: Delete stray spaces, or wrap references in TRIM to strip them: =TRIM(B2).
Convert text-numbers back to numbers (Data > Text to Columns > Finish is a fast fix).
Use a function that ignores text: replace =A2+B2+C2 with =SUM(A2:C2), which skips text cells.

body.richText: If you just want to hide the error while you investigate, wrap the formula in IFERROR: =IFERROR(A2+B2, 0). Use this sparingly — hiding an error is not the same as fixing the data behind it.

richText: A #VALUE! often appears inside a larger formula, such as an
. A different error, #REF!, has its own cause and cure — see

items.question: Why does SUM work but + gives #VALUE!?
items.answerRichText: SUM ignores cells that contain text, while the + operator does not — it tries to add the text and fails. Switching from A2+B2+C2 to SUM(A2:C2) is often the quickest fix.

items.question: How do I find every #VALUE! at once?
items.answerRichText: Press Ctrl+F, search for #VALUE! and set "Look in" to Values. Excel lists every cell showing the error so you can work through them.
